properties.Test Case: paste invalid XML into a page (see note in this cell) (e.g., unmatched <div, invalid &am...). You should just see the raw stuff you pasted. (Or if the problem is bad enough that the file can't be parsed, Bloom will restore a backup.)

properties.Test Case: See how it handles missing images. Currently content pages show a standard "this picture, <whatever>, is missing or was loading too slowly". Cover page just shows a blank space. Either is fine.
properties.Steps: - See how it handles missing images. Currently content pages show a standard "this picture, <whatever>, is missing or was loading too slowly". Cover page just shows a blank space. Either is fine.

properties.Test Case: See how it handles corrupt (e.g., badly hand-edited) HTML and CSS. Not too picky about exactly what happens, but: it shouldn't crash. It should still be possible to work on other books. It should be possible to delete the broken one. There should be an indication of why you can't open it. If you've worked on the book enough to have a backup, it should automatically restore it with a message like this.
